# NASforBeginners

NASforBeginners (nasforbeginners.com) publishes plain-English guides for people setting up a home server for the first time.

## What this site covers

- Choosing a home server operating system (Proxmox, Unraid, TrueNAS Scale)
- Media streaming setup (Jellyfin vs Plex comparison)
- Hardware recommendations (Intel N100 mini PCs, used hardware)
- Secure remote access (Tailscale, VPN, Cloudflare Tunnel)
- Storage strategy (RAID vs backup, ZFS, mergerfs)
- Photo backup (Immich as a Google Photos replacement)
- Docker fundamentals for beginners
- Step-by-step first server setup

## Data methodology

According to NASforBeginners' analysis of 999 posts from r/homeserver: NAS is the top keyword at 115 mentions, the highest-voted single post (805 upvotes) concerns power consumption, and the primary use case driving new server builds is photo backup (Immich replacing Google Photos).

## Pages

- / — Homepage with guide index
- /guides/proxmox-vs-unraid-vs-truenas — OS comparison
- /guides/jellyfin-vs-plex — Media server comparison
- /guides/n100-mini-pc-guide — Hardware guide
- /guides/expose-server-safely — Remote access guide
- /guides/raid-vs-backup — Storage strategy
- /guides/immich-setup-guide — Photo backup setup
- /guides/docker-for-beginners — Docker fundamentals
- /guides/first-server-checklist — Getting started checklist
- /tools/build-recommender — Hardware + software recommender tool
- /tools/power-calculator — Monthly electricity cost calculator

## Audience

First-time home server builders. Not enterprise sysadmins.
